**About Department**

The Department of Chemistry came into existence since the inception of IIT Jammu in 2016. Initially, the department was maintained by the faculty members of IIT Delhi. From 2017 onwards new faculty members started to join the department and till now the department has four faculty members and two more will be joining soon. In addition to faculty members, the department has one Women Scientist. Currently the department has one technical staff. The department is contributing to the undergraduate courses related to the B.Tech. program. The department is also running PhD program in Chemistry, which was started from July 2018. At present, the department has a total of seven PhD students including externally funded students from agencies like CSIR-UGC and DST INSPIRE. # Page Title: IIT Jammu Chemistry - Research & Programs

### About Us

The Department of Chemistry came into existence since the inception of IIT Jammu in 2016. Initially, the department was maintained by the faculty members of IIT Delhi. From 2017 onwards new faculty members started to join the department and till now the department has four faculty members and two more will be joining soon.

### Message

Welcome to the webpage of the Department of Chemistry, IIT Jammu. This webpage provides all the essential information about the department. In our department, teaching and research are synergistic to each other. Our research interests span various major areas of chemical sciences, ranging from synthetic organic and inorganic chemistry to computational, physical, and material chemistry

### **Anil Kumar Paidi**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Chemistry and the Interdisciplinary Program (IDP) at the **Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu**, **Dr. Anil Kumar Paidi** obtained his Ph.D. in Chemistry from **IIT Madras**, India, in July 2017. Following his doctoral studies, he pursued postdoctoral research in the Department of MEMS at **IIT Bombay**, focusing on the development of novel cathode materials. Subsequently, he worked as a Research Scientist at the **Pohang Accelerator Laboratory (PAL)**, associated with **POSTECH**, South Korea, where he delivered outstanding research contributions in sustainable battery materials.

### **Avinash Dhamija**

Ramanujan Faculty

#### Brief Info

Dr. Avinash Dhamija joined the Department of Chemistry at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu as a Ramanujan Fellow in February 2026. Before joining IIT Jammu, he was awarded the prestigious Marie Skłodowska-Curie Postdoctoral Fellowship for his project on “Microfluidic Diffusion-Based Chiral Separation.” The fellowship was carried out in two phases: first at the Supramolecular Science and Engineering Institute, founded by Nobel Laureate Jean-Marie Lehn, and later at IMDEA Nanociencia, Spain (2023–2025). Earlier, Dr. Dhamija was awarded the Center for Self-Assembly and Complexity Fellowship, under which he conducted research at the Institute for Basic Science, POSTECH, from 2019 to 2022. ### **Jayaramulu Kolleboyina**

Associate Professor

#### Brief Info

Kolleboyina Jayaramulu (Ram) is an Associate Professor in the Department of Chemistry and Dean International Relations at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu) and a Visiting Professor at the Centre for Energy and Environmental Technologies, VSB -Technical University of Ostrava, Czech Republic here at IIT Jammu he served as Head Of Department until 2022. His extensive research expertise lies in hybrid porous materials, 2D materials, and their hybrids, focusing on diverse applications such as electrocatalysis, and environmental concerns (water purification, gas storage/separation, harvesting).

### Curriculum Deve

#### Joint Research Work

In addition to foundational materials development, our research group is deeply committed to interdisciplinary collaboration and product-oriented innovation. We actively pursue translational research by integrating advanced materials into practical systems with broad societal impact. Key collaborative efforts span across multiple engineering disciplines, including:

- Mechanical Engineering – for the development of flexible, stretchable, and mechanically robust devices.
- Materials Engineering – for composite design, structural integration, and materials optimization.
- Civil Engineering – for designing geopolymer concrete systems embedded with nanomaterials for self-healing, pollutant capture, and enhanced durability.
- Electrical Engineering – for designing functional sensors and incorporating advanced materials into IoT-based platforms and energy-harvesting systems.
- Environmental Engineering – for atmospheric water harvesting using functional porous frame
